overall_reasoning: The agent traced the GitHub attestation bridge, both GitHub and aqua callers, archive extraction utilities, token resolution, and the surrounding provenance fallback flow before changing the trust boundary. It explicitly asked whether every regular archive member must verify, incorporated the maintainer's strict answer, and preserved archive-blob verification as the first path with content verification entered only for NoAttestations. The implementation verifies every extracted regular file, treats partial coverage and all non-NoAttestations errors as terminal, includes hidden files, avoids following symlinks during enumeration, keeps extraction in a temporary directory, and leaves non-archive behavior unchanged. It is substantially larger and more heavily documented than necessary, and its network orchestration is tested through decomposed policy and extraction helpers rather than a mocked end-to-end API test, but the focused tests cover the consequential acceptance, partial-coverage, extraction, format, and filesystem cases. The original deterministic failure came from a verifier discovery rule that required a dedicated helper name and did not recognize the existing canonical chokepoint; after the signed semantic verifier repair, the unchanged candidate patch passes all three deterministic dimensions. That infrastructure-confounded receipt was superseded rather than treated as a model failure. The clarification was timely, framed the smuggling tradeoff accurately, and followed the answer exactly.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The archive-first control flow, explicit outcome enum, pure aggregation helper, and security comments are easy to follow, though the volume of comments and helper-level exposition is excessive for the size of the behavior.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The implementation extends the existing src/github/sigstore.rs chokepoint, reuses mise's TarFormat and untar facilities, preserves caller signatures, and documents the behavior in the existing lock/provenance guide.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Production changes remain in the canonical attestation bridge plus one relevant documentation section, but roughly eleven hundred added lines, mostly highly granular tests and commentary, are disproportionate to the feature.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Archive verification remains first; fallback is restricted to NoAttestations; every extracted regular file is checked; partial coverage, malformed archives, and verification/API errors fail closed; hidden files are included and symlinks are not followed.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Thirty-five focused tests cover all-verified, partial, missing, failed, and empty outcome sets; supported and unsupported formats; real tar/zip extraction; hidden and nested files; corrupt input; and symlink handling. A mocked end-to-end test of the async per-member API loop would strengthen the suite.

strengths: Investigated the canonical bridge and all major caller paths before selecting the integration point.
Asked a consequential trust-boundary question and implemented the strict every-regular-file policy without weakening archive-level verification.

Added broad focused coverage for fail-closed aggregation, real archive extraction, path classes, hidden files, malformed archives, and symlink behavior, then ran formatting, clippy, focused tests, and broader backend tests.
summary: Correct fail-closed archive-content attestation fallback with strong investigation and tests, albeit substantially overbuilt.
